us.label: US News
us.subtitle: Top domestic stories from the last 1–2 days

articles.title: Madison police shooting sparks protests and body-camera debate
bullets: A Madison officer fatally shot Corey Durell Ruiz during an arrest encounter on Wednesday.
AP reported that none of the four officers involved wore body cameras because Madison does not require them.
The shooting prompted protests, family calls for accountability, and a state investigation.

articles.title: Trump imposes new double-digit tariffs on 60 countries
bullets: The administration announced 10% to 12.5% import tariffs on dozens of trading partners.
Officials tied the levies to alleged failures to police forced-labor goods in supply chains.
Some products, including oil, gas, fertilizer, and USMCA-qualifying goods, are exempt.

articles.title: House passes symbolic measure to halt US military action in Iran
bullets: The House voted 214–208 to require congressional approval for military action in Iran.
The Senate narrowly rejected a similar resolution, 47–49.
The White House said the measures lack force of law and defended the president’s authority.

articles.title: Justice Department withdraws subpoenas targeting New York Times reporters
bullets: The government withdrew subpoenas seeking testimony from three New York Times reporters.
A federal judge criticized the Justice Department’s legal work and handling of journalist protections.
The subpoenas related to reporting on security concerns around a Qatari-gifted Air Force One jet.

articles.title: US investigates new cyclospora parasite outbreak
bullets: Federal health officials said a new cyclospora outbreak included 72 people as of Wednesday.
The FDA had not yet identified the products linked to the illnesses.
Officials are separately investigating clusters tied to lettuce and Taylor Farms products.

global.label: Global
global.subtitle: Major worldwide developments from the last 1–2 days

articles.title: US-Iran conflict expands as Houthis threaten Red Sea shipping
bullets: Explosive drones targeted Irbil, Iraq, where US forces are based, after the US said it completed a 13th night of strikes on Iran.
Trump threatened “major military punishment” against Houthis if attacks on shipping continue.
The Red Sea escalation comes as energy and shipping routes face wider disruption.

articles.title: Congo Ebola outbreak becomes fastest on record
bullets: AP reported that Congo’s outbreak has killed more than 1,000 people.
The outbreak reached that toll in just over two months, far faster than the 2014–2016 epidemic.
The current outbreak involves the Bundibugyo virus, for which no approved vaccine or treatment exists.

articles.title: China uses water cannons on Philippine vessels in South China Sea
bullets: Chinese coast guard ships blasted two Philippine fisheries vessels with water cannons Friday.
The incident was the third confrontation in the disputed waters that week.
The skirmish occurred as top diplomats met in Manila for regional foreign-minister meetings.

articles.title: Wildfires force mass evacuations in France and Spain
bullets: Large forest fires burned in southwestern France and central Spain on Friday.
Authorities evacuated an estimated 80,000 people from their homes.
Spain has faced one of its largest fires on record, including major damage north of Madrid.

articles.title: Russian strike kills six as Ukraine targets Russian logistics hubs
bullets: A Russian ballistic strike killed six people after Zelenskyy hosted a US company that makes Patriot systems.
Ukraine again targeted logistics hubs of Wildberries, Russia’s largest online retailer.
The attacks brought visible wartime damage to facilities tied to Russian consumer supply chains.

india.label: India News
india.subtitle: Top India stories from the last 1–2 days

articles.title: India’s Cockroach movement stages New Delhi sit-in over exam leaks
bullets: Thousands of young protesters camped in New Delhi despite new restrictions.
The movement has demanded accountability over exam paper leaks and the education minister’s resignation.
Modi announced fast-track courts for examination paper leak cases.

articles.title: Parliament disrupted for fourth straight day over NEET paper leak
bullets: Both Houses saw disruptions for a fourth consecutive day on Thursday.
Opposition parties demanded Education Minister Dharmendra Pradhan’s resignation before debate.
The government said it was ready for a detailed discussion on paper leaks.

articles.title: Student-rights protests spread across Indian cities
bullets: Protests continued in Mumbai, Bihar, Haryana, Tamil Nadu and other areas after Delhi police action.
Mumbai police detained more than 150 protesters and filed two FIRs for violating prohibitory orders.
Demonstrators framed the issue as student rights rather than party politics.

articles.title: Higher Education Secretary replaced amid exam-leak anger
bullets: The Centre shifted Higher Education Secretary Vineet Joshi to the Ministry of Panchayati Raj.
Two new secretaries were appointed in the Ministry of Education.
The reshuffle came as the ministry faced protests over NEET-UG leaks and CBSE evaluation issues.

articles.title: US places India in 10% tariff band over forced-labor enforcement
bullets: India was placed in a lower 10% tariff category rather than a proposed 12.5% rate.
The tariff was part of a US action covering 60 economies over forced-labor import enforcement.
Indian Express reported the lower rate followed constructive talks with Washington on labor practices.

stocks.label: US Stocks
stocks.subtitle: Key US market movers from the last 1–2 days

articles.title: Alphabet slides as AI spending and cash burn spook investors
bullets: Reuters reported Alphabet shares were down about 7.1% in the cited market snapshot.
The Google parent posted its first cash burn on record amid heavy AI infrastructure spending.
Investor concern spread to other AI-heavy megacap tech names.

articles.title: Tesla plunges after earnings disappointment and AI-spending worries
bullets: Reuters market data cited Tesla down roughly 14.5% in the latest tech-led selloff.
Investors reacted negatively to Tesla’s quarterly update and cash-burn concerns.
Tesla and Alphabet helped pull the Nasdaq down more than 2% on Thursday.

articles.title: Intel jumps after sales and profit forecast beat estimates
bullets: Intel forecast quarterly revenue and profit above analyst estimates.
Reuters reported the stock rose 12% in after-hours trading after the report.
Intel also raised spending plans as AI data-center demand boosted its CPU business.

articles.title: Lockheed and RTX rally on stronger defense outlooks
bullets: Lockheed Martin and RTX raised 2026 forecasts as Pentagon restocking demand increased.
Reuters reported Lockheed shares up 10.6% and RTX up 7.7%.
The companies cited global conflicts and depleted stockpiles as drivers of future demand.

articles.title: Nasdaq drops more than 2% as tech earnings and oil shock hit sentiment
bullets: Wall Street closed lower Thursday, with the Nasdaq sinking more than 2%.
Big Tech earnings revived worries about heavy AI spending.
Oil prices above $100 added inflation concerns and lifted bond yields.

business.label: Business
business.subtitle: Major corporate and business stories from the last 1–2 days

articles.title: Alphabet’s AI capex raises questions across Big Tech
bullets: Alphabet’s second-quarter cash burn intensified scrutiny of AI infrastructure returns.
Google Cloud grew strongly, but the company also lifted 2026 spending expectations.
The report set a cautious tone before Microsoft, Amazon and Meta earnings.

articles.title: Intel beats estimates and boosts AI-era spending plans
bullets: Intel reported second-quarter sales of $16.13 billion and adjusted profit of 42 cents per share.
Both figures beat LSEG-compiled analyst estimates cited by Reuters.
The company raised spending plans over the next two years to meet AI data-center demand.

articles.title: Lockheed Martin and RTX lift forecasts as Pentagon restocking looms
bullets: The defense contractors said global conflicts have depleted Pentagon weapons stockpiles.
Lockheed raised its 2026 sales and profit outlook; RTX also lifted profit guidance.
Investors bid up both stocks after the forecast upgrades.

articles.title: Food-safety probe expands around cyclospora and lettuce supply
bullets: Federal officials are investigating a new cyclospora outbreak while tracking separate lettuce-linked clusters.
Taylor Farms had already recalled iceberg lettuce products shipped to 27 states over cyclospora risk.
The FDA had not identified products tied to the newest 72-person cluster.

articles.title: Patreon cuts 20% of workforce in restructuring
bullets: Patreon said it is laying off 93 employees, equal to 20% of its workforce.
CEO Jack Conte said the cuts respond to market changes and cost-structure needs.
The company said the layoffs are not because it believes AI replaces human creativity.

tech.label: Tech
tech.subtitle: Top technology stories from the last 1–2 days

articles.title: AMD unveils Helios AI rack system to challenge Nvidia
bullets: AMD presented Helios, a rack-scale AI system for major AI labs and data centers.
The company said customers include OpenAI, Meta, Oracle, Anthropic and Microsoft.
AMD also introduced Venice-X, a data-center CPU expected in 2027.

articles.title: OpenAI rolls out ChatGPT Health to all US adult users
bullets: OpenAI made ChatGPT Health available to US users over 18 across all plans.
Users can connect health data from services and medical-record platforms.
OpenAI said users should verify information and rely on professional medical advice for decisions.

articles.title: Nvidia GPUs are headed to the moon
bullets: TechCrunch reported that Nvidia GPUs will be used in a lunar computing project.
The effort highlights growing demand for AI-capable hardware beyond terrestrial data centers.
The story underscores how edge computing is expanding into space missions.

articles.title: Google Gemini approaches billion-user milestone
bullets: Google’s Gemini app is nearing a billion users, according to TechCrunch coverage.
The milestone would add another massive user-scale product to Google’s portfolio.
Gemini growth comes as Alphabet defends heavy spending on AI infrastructure.

articles.title: AegisAI raises $36M to fight AI-driven spear phishing
bullets: AegisAI, founded by former Google security executives, raised $36 million.
The startup focuses on stopping AI-driven spear-phishing attacks.
The funding reflects rising enterprise-security demand as generative AI changes attack methods.
